Condition-Based Maintenance at Scale

Part 4: Technology Implementation

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

Technology Considerations

Selecting the right condition monitoring technology is critical to program success. This final article in our series covers the key technology decisions.

Core Technology Selection

Evaluate technologies based on:

  • Equipment types in your fleet
  • Accessibility and installation requirements
  • Cost per asset and total cost of ownership
  • Ease of integration with existing systems
  • Scalability and support for enterprise deployment

Electrical Signature Analysis

ESA offers unique advantages for broad fleet monitoring: no sensors needed, works with existing electrical infrastructure, and provides early detection of most failure modes affecting motor-driven equipment.

System Architecture

Your implementation should include:

  • Data collection infrastructure (edge devices or cloud-based)
  • Centralized analytics platform
  • Alert management and notification systems
  • Integration with maintenance management systems
  • Reporting and visualization dashboards

Conclusion

Successful condition-based maintenance transformation requires aligned strategy across Planning, People, Process, and Technology. When all four are optimized, the results are dramatic improvements in reliability, reduced costs, and a competitive advantage in your market.
